name: Text-to-Speech (AVSpeechSynthesizer)
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Text-to-Speech (AVSpeechSynthesizer)

APPROACH:
- Use AVSpeechSynthesizer with AVSpeechUtterance to speak text aloud.
- No special permissions required — TTS works immediately.
- Works in Simulator and on device.

BASIC USAGE:
1. Create an AVSpeechSynthesizer instance — keep a STRONG REFERENCE (store as a property, not a local variable). If the synthesizer is deallocated, speech stops immediately.
2. Create AVSpeechUtterance(string: "Hello world").
3. Set utterance.voice = AVSpeechSynthesisVoice(language: "en-US") (or other locale).
4. Optionally set utterance.rate (0.0–1.0, default ~0.5), utterance.pitchMultiplier (0.5–2.0), utterance.volume (0.0–1.0).
5. Call synthesizer.speak(utterance).

CONTROLS:
- synthesizer.pauseSpeaking(at: .immediate) or .word — pause at current point or end of current word.
- synthesizer.continueSpeaking() — resume after pause.
- synthesizer.stopSpeaking(at: .immediate) — stop completely.
- Check synthesizer.isSpeaking and synthesizer.isPaused for UI state.

VOICE SELECTION:
- AVSpeechSynthesisVoice.speechVoices() returns all available voices.
- Each voice has .language, .name, .quality (.default, .enhanced, .premium).
- Let users pick from available voices grouped by language.
- Enhanced/premium voices sound much better but may need to be downloaded.

HIGHLIGHTING SPOKEN TEXT:
- Implement AVSpeechSynthesizerDelegate.
- speechSynthesizer(_:willSpeakRangeOfSpeechString:utterance:) gives you the NSRange of the word about to be spoken.
- Use this to highlight the current word in the UI (convert NSRange to String.Index for SwiftUI).

AUDIO SESSION:
- If the app also records or plays other audio, configure AVAudioSession category to .playback or .playAndRecord before speaking.
- For simple TTS-only apps, the default session works fine.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT create AVSpeechSynthesizer as a local variable in a function — it gets deallocated and speech stops. Store it as a class property.
- Do NOT call synthesizer.speak() with the same utterance object twice — create a new AVSpeechUtterance each time.
- Do NOT set rate to 0.0 — it means "minimum speed" which is extremely slow. Use 0.4–0.6 for natural speech.
- Do NOT forget to stop speaking when the view disappears (implement onDisappear cleanup).
- Do NOT assume all voices are available offline — some enhanced voices require download.
antiPatterns: Creating AVSpeechSynthesizer as a local variable (gets deallocated, speech stops)
Reusing the same AVSpeechUtterance object for multiple speak() calls
Setting rate to 0.0 (extremely slow, effectively broken)
Not stopping speech when the view disappears
Assuming all voices are available offline
